article.title: Secure Business AI Assistant: What to Keep Private

blog.title: Secure Business AI Assistant: What to Keep Private

facebook.caption:
Secure Business AI Assistant: What to Keep Private

A practical guide to deciding which client records, SOPs, financial details, and lead data should stay out of public AI tools before a business assistant goes live.

Read it: https://eb28.co/blog/secure-business-ai-assistant-what-to-keep-private-from-day-one/

instagram.caption:
Secure AI starts with what stays private.

Client records. SOPs. Pricing. Lead data. Internal decisions. Put boundaries around the first workflow before connecting more tools.

Read the full guide at eb28.co/blog/
instagram.creativeBrief: Carousel: Slide 1 secure AI starts with private boundaries, Slide 2 data that stays out, Slide 3 one controlled workflow, Slide 4 source citations and review, Slide 5 CTA to EB28 project brief.

x.caption:
Secure Business AI Assistant: What to Keep Private

Start with one controlled workflow, approved documents, source visibility, access rules, logs, and human review before connecting private AI to lead follow-up.

https://eb28.co/blog/secure-business-ai-assistant-what-to-keep-private-from-day-one/

linkedin.caption:
EB28 growth brief refresh: Secure Business AI Assistant: What to Keep Private

For small businesses, the safest private-AI starting point is not every company file. It is one workflow, clear data boundaries, retrieval from approved material, logging, citations, and human review before customer-facing decisions.

https://eb28.co/blog/secure-business-ai-assistant-what-to-keep-private-from-day-one/

shortFormVideo.hook: Before you build a business AI assistant, decide what it is never allowed to see.
shortFormVideo.brief: 15-25 second vertical video. Open with a prompt box over sensitive business files, then three beats: keep private data out, start with one workflow, require citations plus review. End on the EB28 article title and project brief CTA.
shortFormVideo.caption: Secure business AI starts with private boundaries, not bigger prompts.
